rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion And Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 29, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s false representation and civil battery causes of action and claim for punitive damages are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his claims against defendant. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his contention of liability against it precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Westburne Supply Inc.S
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 29, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Westburne Supply Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs¿ claims against it are barred by the statute of limitations, C.C.P. 340.2. Plaintiffs¿ First Amended Complaint for survival, wrongful death was filed within the statute of limitations period and defendant failed to demonstrate that the amendment substituting it into this action does not relate back to the filing of the First Amended Complaint. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Monterey Mechanical Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings, Or In The Alternative, Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 29, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant Frederick Meiswinkel, Inc.¿s joinder to Monterey Mechanical Company¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings or, in the alternative, motion to dismiss is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the doctrine of collateral estoppel operates as an estoppel or conclusive adjudication as to plaintiff¿s claims. The Court did not consider the motion for judgment on the pleadings. (C.C.P. 438(e).) Local Rule 8.2 does not bar this Court from hearing the motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL JIMENEZ VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Therma Corporation'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 29, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 4, Defendant Therma Corp.¿s motion to dismiss plaintiff¿s complaint is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
